title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: An internal Python game release engineer. They need replayable simulation, versioned settings, a pure core, layered unit and headless SDL tests, representative profiles, licensed packaged assets, PyInstaller builds produced on each supported OS, and clean offline launch evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Deterministic Tested Cross-Platform Release Candidate with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Model state transitions as deterministic functions over state, actions, time step, and injected randomness where practical.
Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Input Replay
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 2398 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Input Replay.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Input Replay, keep Input Replay connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Platform Matrix
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-platform-matrix-1yrhi6" that uses case 9782 to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Platform Matrix.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Platform Matrix into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Platform Matrix, keep Platform Matrix connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-platform-matrix-1yrhi6
# Competency: asteroids-platform-matrix.
# Case 9782: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_platform_matrix_1yrhi6(target_platforms: int = 3, clean_installs: int = 3, declared_assets: int = 12, packaged_assets: int = 12, licenses_recorded: bool = True, offline_launch: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1yrhi6 = "1yrhi6-6ihry1-9782"
    assert evidence_variant_1yrhi6
    evidence_axes_1yrhi6 = (
        "player1yrhi6",
        "seed9782",
        "input6ihry1",
        "frame1yrhi69782",
        "state97821yrhi6",
        "playtest6ihry19782",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1yrhi6)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or clean_installs < 0:
        return False, target_platforms, "package-domain"
    if clean_installs != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_installs, "platform-gap"
    if packaged_assets != declared_assets:
        return False, packaged_assets, "asset-gap"
    if not licenses_recorded or not offline_launch:
        return False, packaged_assets, "release-input"
    assert packaged_assets == declared_assets
    return True, target_platforms, "portable-build"
